question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of message payload size limits in listen() to receive extremely large TopicMessage payloads that exhaust client-side or server-side memory during streaming?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not enforce the maxPageSize or maxBufferSize from ListenerProperties at the CompositeTopicListener level, allowing the underlying listener to return more data than the configured limits?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopicListener() throws UnsupportedOperationException with the type value embedded in the message — \

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not implement circuit breaking or fallback logic, so if the underlying listener (e.g., Redis) becomes unavailable, all subscriptions fail with unhandled exceptions that crash the reactive pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of subscription deduplication in listen() to create multiple identical subscriptions for the same topicId and startTime, causing the server to process and deliver the same messages multiple times and amplify resource usage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that filterMessage() uses object equality (equals()) for topicId comparison without verifying the EntityId implementation's equals() contract, potentially causing incorrect topic matching if EntityId.equals() has a bug or is overridden insecurely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not implement any form of message deduplication, allowing a replay attack where the same TopicMessage is delivered multiple times if the underlying listener emits duplicates?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not validate the subscriberId field in TopicMessageFilter, allowing an attacker to set a crafted subscriberId that collides with another user's subscriberId and potentially interfere with their subscription state in shared listener implementations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any timeout on the listen() Flux to keep a subscription alive indefinitely even after the client disconnects, causing a resource leak of server-side subscription objects?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that recordMetric() records a metric for every single message delivered via doOnNext, causing the MeterRegistry to accumulate an unbounded number of timer samples and exhaust heap memory in a high-throughput scenario?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not enforce any global concurrent subscription limit, allowing a single attacker to exhaust all available gRPC server worker threads by holding many long-lived subscriptions open simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not check whether the topicId in the filter corresponds to a public or private topic, allowing access to topics that should be restricted based on topic visibility rules?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that filterMessage() does not validate the integrity of the TopicMessage object (e.g., no signature verification), allowing a compromised backend to inject forged messages that pass the filter and are delivered to subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not implement any form of output encoding or sanitization on TopicMessage fields before streaming them, allowing stored XSS or injection payloads in message content to be delivered to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not enforce the prefetch value from ListenerProperties at the CompositeTopicListener level, allowing the reactive pipeline to request an unbounded number of items from the upstream publisher and overwhelm the data source?
